hook.text: Women do two-thirds of the world's work but earn a fraction of its income. Gender is a system of stratification.

buildup.text: Gender stratification is the unequal distribution of wealth, power, and privilege between men and women in a society.

discovery.text: It varies across cultures. Some societies offer women more autonomy; others restrict them to domestic roles entirely.

twist.text: Patriarchy isn't universal. Some matrilineal societies give women control over property, children, and the household.

climax.text: Gender stratification reveals that inequality between men and women is cultural, not natural. It can be changed.

punchline.text: Gender inequality is made by culture, not by nature.

quiz.question: What does anthropology reveal about gender stratification?
options: It varies across cultures and is culturally constructed
Men and women are equal in all societies
